turns.text: Here's the first frame from the surveillance feed.
turns.image_description: Surveillance footage showing a person standing in the center of the frame.

turns.text: The subject is initially positioned in the center of the frame. I'll keep that as our reference point.
This is the next frame. There's a shadow now, but I don't think the person moved.
turns.image_description: Same scene with a shadow from a tree in the lower left corner, no change to the person's position.

turns.text: Confirmed, the shadow is a new element but the person remains centered. No positional change detected.
Now the person seems to have moved. They're near the wall on the right side.
turns.image_description: Person moves to the right side of the frame, now near the wall.

turns.text:
Where is the person located in the final frame?

B) Lower right corner with partial occlusion
A) Center position
C) Upper right corner facing the camera
D) Near the wall on the right side

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: Turn 1 establishes the initial center position. Turn 2 confirms the person remains centered despite a shadow. Turn 3 shows movement to the right wall. Turn 4 updates to upper right corner. Turn 5 reveals the final position in lower right with occlusion.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The person's path involves multiple directional changes that are only coherent when viewed in sequence, requiring the model to track intermediate positions.
